query: "gas prices" OR "gasoline prices" OR "oil prices" OR petrol

articles.title: Oil prices to hit $120 soon? Goldman Sachs makes big prediction as Hormuz concerns loom
articles.description: Goldman Sachs has warned Brent crude could spike to $120 per barrel if disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz persist, though its base case assumes easing Middle East tensions and prices averaging $80 in Q4.

articles.title: India's ethanol mission sees state-run fuel firms spend Rs 1.72 lakh crore
articles.description: Public Sector OMCs spent over Rs 48,000 crore on ethanol procurement in ESY 2023-24. They procured Rs 73,996 crore worth of ethanol during ESY 2024-25. As of July 2026, 501 ethanol manufacturing units were registered for supply. Uttar Pradesh led ethanol-blended petrol sales with 124.98 crore litres. The domestic procurement network supports the Ethanol Blended Petrol Programme nationwide.

articles.title: IMF says oil prices, weak monsoon pose biggest risks to India's FY27 GDP growth
articles.description: India's GDP growth in 2026/27 faces key downside risks from a ‌widening war in the Middle East that is driving up oil prices and a monsoon weakened by the El Nino weather effect, an International Monetary Fund official said.

articles.title: Conflict sees gas prices rise again to $4 average
articles.description: After a brief respite, the price of gasoline in the United States is back on the rise.

articles.title: Oil prices, weak monsoon pose biggest risks to India's FY27 GDP growth, says IMF
articles.description: India's economic growth faces significant risks from a widening Middle East conflict. Rising oil prices and a potentially weak monsoon pose challenges for the nation. The International Monetary Fund has slightly adjusted its growth forecasts for upcoming years. The Fund will also reassess India's national accounts quality later this year. These assessments follow concerns raised about the country's statistical methods.

articles.title: Rupee firms as oil retreats, traders gauge flow momentum under RBI measures
articles.description: The Indian rupee strengthened on Tuesday, benefiting from falling oil prices. Optimism over potential mediation in the U.S.-Iran conflict also aided sentiment. Strong inflows tied to the central bank's recent policy measures further supported the currency. These measures have attracted over twenty billion dollars in about a month. Regional Asian currencies and stocks also saw modest gains after oil prices dipped.

articles.title: Oil Prices Dip Amid US-Iran Tensions and Houthi Threats
articles.description: Oil prices dipped on Tuesday as markets considered potential diplomacy between the U.S. and Iran amidst a new wave of conflicts, including Houthi threats to Saudi Arabia. Brent crude futures fell to $88.26 per barrel, while U.S. crude dipped to $82.50 per barrel. A 10-day ceasefire proposal is under review.

articles.title: India's Ethanol Procurement Surge: A Key Player in Blended Petrol Programme
articles.description: Public Sector Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) have significantly increased their expenditure on ethanol procurement to support the Ethanol Blended Petrol Programme. By mid-2026, 501 manufacturing units were registered, contributing to the rise of domestic ethanol production and consumption, with Uttar Pradesh leading in ethanol-blended petrol sales.

articles.title: Goldman Warns Oil Could Hit $120 as Middle East War Drags On
articles.description: Crude oil prices could surge to $120 per barrel towards the end of the year if the war in the Middle East extends and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed for

articles.title: Oil Reverses Gains on Renewed Hope of Peace in Iran
articles.description: Crude oil prices reversed their climb this morning, retreating on reports that efforts are being made to negotiate another ceasefire between the United States
